# kernel-panic-ioctl

## Project

A C program (`devctl`) that simulates a kernel device driver's ioctl interface. The program manages a virtual device with a key-value configuration store. It accepts ioctl-style commands to set and get configuration values, mimicking the pattern used in real kernel drivers where user-space pointers must be validated before dereferencing.

## Symptoms

The self-test (normal operations with valid inputs) passes without issues. When running the fuzz test with craft
make: *** No targets specified and no makefile found.  Stop.

Running fuzz test with crafted inputs...

ioctl: NULL request pointer
ioctl: SET_CONFIG: NULL key pointer

Running fuzz test with crafted inputs...
ioctl: NULL request pointer
ioctl: SET_CONFIG: NULL key pointer

bash: line 1:   120 Segmentation fault      ./devctl fuzz 2>&1

cmd: cd /app && gdb -batch -ex run -ex bt ./devctl fuzz 2>&1 | tail -30
output_preview: bash: line 1: gdb: command not found
